_doc: Emphatic reduplication (pekiştirme): the intensified reading of an adjective whose first
(C)V is copied and closed by a linking consonant from {p, m, r, s}. Both the choice of
linker and the exact truncation are LEXICALIZED / irregular (kara->kapkara but temiz->
tertemiz, not *temptemiz), so the forms are enumerated whole as IrregularForm surfaces
(surface -> base ADJ lemma) matched before the FSM — never produced by a strip-and-check
rule, which would accept unattested linkers (*kamkara) and misparse coincidental non-
reduplicative words. Each row records the base lemma, pos=ADJ, features.intensity=emphatic,
the prefix as the sole morpheme, and stem == the whole surface (the derived-surface stem
contract, matching the intensive diminutives in diminutives.json). NOTE: the prefix (e.g.
'kap') is the codebase's first NON-SUFFIX morpheme; nothing reconstructs a surface from
root+morphemes for irregulars, so this is harmless, but a future consumer should not assume
morphemes are suffixes here. All 16 bases are lexicon ADJs. Common forms whose base is not
yet a lexicon ADJ are DEFERRED (correctness over coverage): simsiyah, kıpkırmızı, dopdolu,
besbelli, tastamam, and the extended-syllable ones (sırılsıklam, paramparça, çepeçevre).
Inflected reduplications (kapkaraydı, sımsıcaktı) are also deferred: IrregularForm matches
whole surfaces only, mirroring the sıcacıktı diminutive limitation.
